is a quadrennial [multi-sport event](/wiki/Multi-sport_event "Multi-sport event") held among athletes from [South Asia](/wiki/South_Asia "South Asia"). The [South Asia Olympic Council](/wiki/South_Asia_Olympic_Council "South Asia Olympic Council"), which was formed in 1983, governs it. The Games consist of seven countries, namely [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh"), [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan "Bhutan"), [India](/wiki/India "India"), [Maldives](/wiki/Maldives "Maldives"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka"). [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan "Afghanistan") had participated in the Games four times, but left the [SAOC](/wiki/South_Asia_Olympic_Council "South Asia Olympic Council") after participating in [2016](/wiki/2016_South_Asian_Games "2016 South Asian Games").

Multi-sport event in South Asia

South Asian Games

The **South Asian Games** is a quadrennial [multi-sport event](/wiki/Multi-sport_event "Multi-sport event") held among athletes from [South Asia](/wiki/South_Asia "South Asia"). The [South Asia Olympic Council](/wiki/South_Asia_Olympic_Council "South Asia Olympic Council"), which was formed in 1983, governs it. The Games consist of seven countries, namely [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh"), [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan "Bhutan"), [India](/wiki/India "India"), [Maldives](/wiki/Maldives "Maldives"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka"). [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan "Afghanistan") had participated in the Games four times, but left the [SAOC](/wiki/South_Asia_Olympic_Council "South Asia Olympic Council") after participating in [2016](/wiki/2016_South_Asian_Games "2016 South Asian Games").

The first South Asian Games were hosted by [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal") in 1984. From 1984 to 1987 they were held every year except 1986, as it was a year of [Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games "Commonwealth Games") and [Asian Games](/wiki/Asian_Games "Asian Games"). From 1987 onwards, they have been held every two years except for some occasions. In 2004, the South Asian Sports Council decided to rename the games from the South Asian Federation Games to the South Asian Games as officials believed the word federation was diminishing the emphasis on the event and acting as a barrier to attracting spectators.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

These are often known as the South Asian version of [Olympic Games](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games"). The [XIII South Asian Games](/wiki/2019_South_Asian_Games "2019 South Asian Games") was held at [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu"), [Pokhara](/wiki/Pokhara "Pokhara") and [Janakpur](/wiki/Janakpur "Janakpur") from 1 December to 10 December 2019.

The South Asian Games is one of five subregional Games of the [Olympic Council of Asia](/wiki/Olympic_Council_of_Asia "Olympic Council of Asia"). The others are [Central Asian Games](/wiki/Central_Asian_Games "Central Asian Games"), [East Asian Youth Games](/wiki/East_Asian_Youth_Games "East Asian Youth Games"), [Southeast Asian Games](/wiki/Southeast_Asian_Games "Southeast Asian Games"), and [West Asian Games](/wiki/West_Asian_Games "West Asian Games").[[2]](#cite_note-2)
